findings.title: Batch-import companions are never persisted to the server, so the next /api/data poll silently deletes them (orphaning every imported expense's payer/split names)
findings.rootCause: runBatchImport mutates activeTrip.companions in local STATE via addTripCompanion(activeTrip, who) and addTripCompanion(activeTrip, name) for split names (upload.ts:390-398), but it NEVER calls upsertTrip(activeTrip). Its only server calls are upsertExpense per row (persists expenses) and a final syncWithServer() (upload.ts:463). syncWithServer() now POSTs an EMPTY body to /api/sync (api.ts:77-81) — it no longer ships trips/companions at all. So the new companions live only in localStorage. On the next pullFromServer, STATE.trips is fully replaced from the server (api.ts:183 `STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...)`), merging back only the 4 heavy media fields — companions come straight from the server, which never received the import's additions. Net: imported roster additions are wiped on the next poll while their expenses persist, breaking the expense→companion→settlement hand-off.

findings.title: One-click 'Settle' on a no-FX-rate currency debt (ARS/EGP/VND…) fabricates a 1:1 euroValue, poisoning EUR balances, the cross-trip Global tab and Insights with a wildly inflated number
findings.rootCause: settleDebt computes `const euroValue = convertCurrency(amount, currency, 'EUR')` (actions.ts:115). For a no-rate currency convertCurrency falls back to rate 1 (currency.ts:71-78 `_rateFor` returns CONVERSION_RATES[code] || 1), so euroValue == the raw foreign amount. PATH A then POSTs {amount, currency:'ARS', euroValue:<raw>}. The server's non-EUR guard (settlements.py:222-228) only rejects when `rate is None AND euro_value is None` — here euro_value is the (bogus) raw number, so it passes; compute_euro_value's cold path returns that client hint verbatim (fx_rates.py:221-228). The settlement is stored with euro_value == raw foreign amount and counts 1:1 as EUR everywhere (applySettlementToBalances euroValue||amount, computeGlobalBalances, settlementEurForTotal, Insights netBalances). SettlementView only consults hasRate(d.currency) to decide whether to show the '≈ EUR' hint (line 302), NOT to gate the Settle button; openManualSettleModal has the same gap.

findings.title: History day-total and 'settled' badge use divergent EUR fallbacks for a non-EUR settlement with no euroValue (raw foreign amount vs €0)
findings.rootCause: settledStatsForTrip uses settlementEurForTotal (viewData.ts:20-24), which deliberately returns 0 for a non-EUR row with falsy euroValue. But collectSettlementHistory builds each HistoryItem.euroValue as `s.euroValue || s.amount || 0` (viewData.ts:174) — the exact unsafe fallback settlementEurForTotal was written to avoid — and SettlementView's HistoryTab sums those (`totalForDay = items.reduce((s,x)=>s+(x.euroValue||0),0)`, SettlementView.tsx:397). The two surfaces therefore compute the same row's EUR contribution two different ways.

findings.title: Batch import splits early rows across a smaller companion roster than later rows, because addTripCompanion grows the roster mid-loop
findings.rootCause: Inside the parsedRows.forEach loop, runBatchImport first calls addTripCompanion(activeTrip, who) for the current row (upload.ts:390), then computes the equal-split default from getTripCompanionNames(activeTrip) (upload.ts:404-408). Because the roster is mutated incrementally as rows are processed, tripRoster.length grows during the loop: row 1 (only 'Alice' known) splits 100% to Alice; row 5 (after Alice, Bob, Carol seen) splits 33/33/33. The split denominators are not stable across the import, so equal-share expenses don't reflect the final companion set.
